description: Retired public claims that must not reappear on public claim surfaces without explicit retraction context. Scanned by tools/check_retired_claims.py (CI gate). Published records (preprint sources, published paper pages) are immutable by owner ruling 2026-07-16 and are exempt: their corrections travel via new Zenodo versions and live-surface amendments, never in-place edits.

claims.reason: Cold-start artefact (un-warmed Qiskit first call); warmed declared-hardware baseline is 96.5x (L=4) / 33.7x (L=8) as a local regression guard, not a published claim. See docs/native_speedup_benchmark.md.

The blanket '>8σ' CHSH statement misattached the significance: the committed counts give 7.54σ for the S=2.165 pair (q0–q1) and 8.94σ for the S=2.188 pair (q2–q3). State exact per-pair significances instead. See scripts/recompute_chsh_bell_test.py.

The IBM v2 'DUAL PROTECTION on IBM hardware (F_FIM > F_XY, p < 10^-6)' finding is retired as a scientific claim, NOT as data. The underlying data has been recovered and published in full (data/ibm_hardware_v2_recovered_2026-07-18/: raw counts + real IBM job identifiers + calibration; committed reproducer scripts/analyse_ibm_v2_recovered.py recomputes 8/9 aggregates to |dmean|<1e-4). F_FIM > F_XY (all-zero survival) is a genuine hardware observation but is NOT evidence of a coherence-protection mechanism: the circuits differ in depth/structure, and the hypothesis was tested properly on the promoted ibm_kingston SCPN/FIM campaign and committed as a NEGATIVE/falsification result (docs/campaigns/scpn_fim_claim_boundary_2026-05-05.md, docs/hardware_status_ledger.md). Simulation/theory 'dual protection' findings (Lyapunov + spectral gap, NB40; FIM enhances MBL, NB31) are not hardware claims and are not retired.
